title: VibeUs Public Life Landing
product_truth: VibeUs is a live social platform where AI-assisted creators publish projects, discuss work, receive reactions, save useful projects and build an author profile.
audience: People who already use AI to make apps, tools, content and experiments but lack a public environment for feedback, discovery and reputation.
primary_task: Move a maker from private completion to public project publication on VibeUs.

primary_action.label: Войти и опубликовать
primary_action.consequence: Opens the real VibeUs authentication and registration route.

confirmed: VibeUs has public feed, catalog, project pages, comments, support, saves and author profiles.
The public catalog currently exposes real project cards and author attribution.
Authentication supports sign in and registration.
assumptions: Publishing is the highest-value first action for a maker arriving from this landing.
The strongest pain is not making with AI but gaining useful human continuation after making.
unknowns: No approved customer quotes or conversion metrics were supplied.
No formal brand book or analytics target was supplied.

references.observed: Live feed, navigation, reactions, people and project cards.
references.inference: The product is a social environment rather than a portfolio directory only.
references.rights_note: Product screenshot used as owner-context evidence only.

references.role: catalog proof and real project names
references.observed: Public catalog with project cards, authors and categories.
references.inference: Real project discovery can substantiate the landing claim.
references.rights_note: Product screenshot used as owner-context evidence only.

references.role: project detail, discussion and author proof
references.observed: Project description, stack, author, reaction and discussion surfaces.
references.inference: A project gains a persistent public context after publication.
references.rights_note: Product screenshot used as owner-context evidence only.

references.observed: Validated local pattern contracts for composition and evidence.
references.inference: Foundation rules can guide structure without donating a visual shell.
references.rights_note: Owned internal method, used as abstract pattern knowledge.

constraints: Greenfield composition with no reuse of previous VibeUs landing shells.
Static GitHub Pages compatible HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
No invented metrics, testimonials or product UI.
Self-hosted Cyrillic-capable font, responsive layout and reduced motion.
No visible em dash or en dash.

hypothesis.visitor_change: From I made something privately to my project can gain a public life and work for my reputation.
hypothesis.visual_law: A private artifact progressively attracts people, reactions and links until it becomes a public object.
hypothesis.dramaturgy: Private result, missing continuation, public lifecycle, real evidence, human network, publication CTA.
identity_deltas: Project lifecycle is the primary narrative instead of a feature grid.
One horizontal sequence is used only to explain irreversible progression.
Original editorial hero photography replaces generic AI imagery.
Real VibeUs screens and project names form the proof layer.
Warm vermilion and raw editorial typography avoid familiar AI neon conventions.

composition.responsive_transform: The desktop horizontal lifecycle becomes a vertical chapter sequence below 900 px and under reduced-motion preference.
composition.proof_cadence: Claim, mechanism, live screenshot and real project, community consequence, action.

proof_plan: Static contract checks and real destination validation.
Desktop screenshots at 1440 px across the full page.
Mobile screenshots at 390 px and overflow check at 320 px.
Console and failed network request capture.
Menu, anchor, reduced-motion and horizontal journey behavior.
All three hero query modes render with the same readable proposition and working actions.
Caster visual acceptance and Anton product trust acceptance.
GitHub Pages build and external HTTP 200.
residual_limits: No conversion analytics or user-test evidence is available before publication.
